Uernth

First off I will agree it’s short, but it looks great, it’s interactive (especially moving the droids around with your pointer like a cat with a laser) and it’s fun. For a free experience if you’re a Star Wars fan you need to try it.
It’s fun but you only repair 3 or 4 droids also it took me like 20 minutes to beat
A cool way to let smaller humans enter the Star Wars galaxy. Mundane for grown ups.
Trash
This is a short game worth playing if you like Star Wars. Repetitive gameplay but the graphics are beautiful.
Fun little experience with funny moments and a little gift at the end

When it comes to the Star Wars brand, any product could be slapped together with substandard quality and would probably still make quite a few bucks. Is that the case here? Simply put, no. My hope with this review, though, is to help anyone reading set their expectations.

It is, of course, merely a promotion in VR for (at the time) the upcoming Last Jedi film. There were many directions they could have gone with this. The focus could have been on Rey, or Kylo Ren, or even one of the classic characters from the original trilogy. They could have charged a premium for something way less than substantial but with a hollow “wow” factor. In other words, they could have baited us into a rip off. Instead, they offer a charming mini game for free, focusing on BB-8 and the droid repair shop whereby the player must quickly diagnose and fix various droids. It’s not exactly enthralling, at least not until a nice suspenseful moment at the end that I will not reveal here. What I liked about this gameplay decision is that, while yes, it’s aimed mostly at younger kids, isn’t that really what Star Wars should be about? I mean, sure, millions of us adults grew up with it as kids, but the legacy for me should be about drawing in newer generations, as opposed to simply placating us old farts by constantly playing up the mythology. I kind of appreciate a Star Wars mini game that values magical moments for young, new viewers over the almighty dollar. Of course, that decision has its own long-lasting implications of financial gain, but that’s just smart business.

Anyway, this review is almost as long as the game itself, but I still recommend it as a neat experience to quench the VR Star Wars thirst while perhaps also being fun and memorable for kids, all at no cost. There’s something to be said for that.
Why is Poe remove in go??????😢

Pretty entertaining for a little while. All you do is repair droids and that's it, nothing else really. I'd really only recommend this to star wars fans though. I doubt anyone who isn't a star wars fan would get much enjoyment out of it. It's fun and it had somewhat of a story in the background, but nothing fancy. Star wars fan or not, it's worth a try.

This is much too short... but, it's free, so that complaint is just me being a choosy beggar. In this game, you are a worker in a charming, little droid bay, and with the assistance of a helper droid and some smaller supply droids, you are tasked with repairing several BB-8-style droids (including a droid that may well be BB-8 itself, as it looks identical).
What I found most impressive is that there is no dialogue (except maybe some background noise on the comms, at one point), yet there is a clear and simple tutorial. That's great game design. Plus, although I wasn't expecting it, there is an inkling of a narrative here. It's simple, but quite effective and I very much enjoyed this, even though it only took about five minutes total.

Even if you aren't a fan of Star Wars, I think you'll find something to enjoy here. But, when it ends, don't say I didn't warn you that this was only a short experience...
Hello. I found this game to be very fun, but when the new movie comes out, could an update with new droids come along? I loved the game, and it would be a nice touch

I loved this soooo much!  I only downloaded it to share with my Dad but I ended up really enjoying it myself.  It's so well done. I can't wait to share this with my Dad; he's a big Star Wars fan :-).
Edited to say my Dad loved this too.  He's 80 years old and never in a million years could he imagine getting to fix robots on a space ship.  I wish I could give this more than 5 stars.
I personally LOVED this game. It was really cool and fun (not to mention free)! I am a huge Star Wars fan so I know a good game when I see one. This one is definitely worth trying out for yourself!
